what_it_does: Renders a Templates canvas for saved reusable templates, decorates them with parent Type registry metadata, displays them in grouped or flat layouts, lets users edit template parameters or variables, run resolved briefs or materialized workshop documents, open the Type Registry, and delete templates.
observations: Uses React hooks aliased with _tp suffix from the global React object.
Subscribes to window.CV_REGISTRY updates and increments local tick state to refresh derived registry-backed data.
Decorates saved templates with parent registry Types using doc.type for the top-level grouping path.
Supports grouped layout by parent Type and flat layout across all saved templates.
TemplateCard distinguishes workshop-doc templates from brief-pattern templates using t.kind === 'workshop-doc'.
Brief templates resolve {{key}} placeholders with current input values before running.
Workshop templates are materialized through window.WS_AI.materializeTemplate before invoking onRunWorkshopTemplate.
Thumbnail rendering is conditional on a faux parent Type and window.TypeThumb.
Several helpers are expected as globals, including CanvasHeader, ViShape, window.CV_REGISTRY, window.TypeLayerBadge, window.TypeThumb, window.WS_AI, and window.dsaToast.
standouts: Registry metadata is used as presentation context rather than stored directly on each rendered group.
The same card UI handles brief templates and workshop-document templates with different run paths.
A faux Type is assembled for thumbnailing by combining the parent Type, template name, document defaults, and template variables.
conventions: Uses file-local React hook aliases rather than direct useState/useMemo/useEffect references.
Attaches the exported component to window.Templates instead of using ES module exports.
Keeps much of the component styling inline while relying on dsa-* and cv-* class names for larger structural styling.
Uses optional chaining for callback invocation and many global integrations.
Uses parameter arrays for brief templates and variable arrays for workshop-doc templates.
concerns: The registry subscription effect depends on window.CV_REGISTRY?.subscribe returning either a cleanup function or undefined, but does not normalize the cleanup path.
window.TypeLayerBadge is called in grouped headers and thumbnails without checking that the global exists in every render path.
The TemplateCard state is initialized from template params and variables once, so later changes to t.params, t.variables, or defaults would not automatically reset input values.
Inline styles make layout and visual behavior harder to reuse or audit within this file.
The placeholder resolver only matches word characters in {{key}} tokens, so parameter keys with dashes, dots, or spaces will not resolve.
notes: This file is the saved-template browsing and rerun surface, binding template cards to registry Types, editable values, and two execution paths.
questions: What exact shape does window.CV_REGISTRY.subscribe return when a subscription is created?
Can parameter or variable keys contain characters outside \w?
Are savedAt values always preformatted strings suitable for direct display?
Should TypeLayerBadge be optional in the same way TypeThumb is optional?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated saved-template and parent-Type grouping purpose matches the implementation; the comment's built-in "Types" mode is not explicit here beyond registry metadata, badges, and an Open Type Registry action.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make generated or workshop-derived artifacts reusable by exposing their variable parts, connecting them back to Type Registry metadata, and allowing reruns with edited values.
novelty: The file treats templates as instances of registry Types and creates a merged faux Type solely for thumbnail rendering.
contribution: This file contributes a neutral reusable-template ledger surface: grouped Type context, editable parameter and variable inputs, template thumbnails, brief placeholder resolution, workshop document materialization, and delete/run actions for saved template instances.
summary_for_embedding: A React Templates canvas renders saved templates from Build or Workshop, groups them by registry parent Type, lets users edit parameters or variables, and reruns them as resolved briefs or materialized workshop documents.
intention_for_embedding: This was for turning saved generated or workshop documents into reusable Type-linked templates that can be inspected, edited, rerun, and managed from one canvas.

symbols.does: Renders the Templates canvas with header actions, empty state, flat layout, or grouped-by-Type layout.
symbols.description: This component subscribes to registry changes, decorates saved templates with parent Type metadata, derives groups from that metadata, and chooses between empty, flat, and grouped render paths. It passes each template to TemplateCard with deletion and run callbacks.

symbols.does: Renders one saved template card with thumbnail, metadata, editable values, run action, and delete action.
symbols.description: TemplateCard initializes editable values from params and variables, determines whether the template is a workshop document, and builds the appropriate input set. It also creates an optional faux Type for thumbnail rendering and presents different fixed-content states for brief and workshop templates without variables.

symbols.does: Runs the current template values through either workshop materialization or brief resolution.
symbols.description: The nested run function branches on the workshop-doc flag. Workshop templates are materialized through window.WS_AI.materializeTemplate and passed to onRunWorkshopTemplate when successful, while brief templates pass the already resolved brief string to onRunBrief.

symbols.does: Replaces {{key}} placeholders in a brief pattern with matching values.
symbols.description: resolveBrief converts the pattern to a string and replaces double-brace word-character tokens using the values object. When a value is missing, it preserves the original placeholder text.
